message:
test(fuzz): add FcpsFrameHeader 114-byte layout + flag-mutex fuzz target (resolves aij5b)

FcpsFrameHeader (fcps.rs:130-275) is the 114-byte fixed-layout FCPS
symbol-plane header with three security gates beyond standard
wire-format rules:
- ERROR + RESPONSE mutex (fcps.rs:211-215)
- STREAM_END requires STREAMING (fcps.rs:216-220)
- symbol_size=0 → InvalidSymbolSize (fcps.rs:233-235)

Existing fuzz_fcps_frame tests decode panic-freedom; this discrete-MR
fuzz is missing. Parallel to obk4f (FcpcFrameHeader) and etzqz
(SymbolRecord layout).

Three properties driven over a sanitized-flags input (mutex pre-resolved
so per-iteration round-trip is well-defined):
- round-trip preserves all 11 fields
- layout binding (11 byte ranges at exact LE positions [0..114))
- TooShort rejection at FCPS_HEADER_LEN - 1

Once-gated anchors verifying each rejection gate:
- (a) symbol_size=0 → InvalidSymbolSize
- (b) ERROR + RESPONSE both set → InvalidFlags
- (c) STREAM_END without STREAMING → InvalidFlags
- (d) acceptance: STREAMING + STREAM_END together MUST decode (keeps
  rejection assertions non-vacuous)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -808,5 +808,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_fcps_header_layout"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fcps_header_layout.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
